name: Juliana Stratton
bio: Juliana Stratton is the Lieutenant Governor of Illinois, serving as J.B. Pritzker's running mate since January 2019. She was born and raised in Chicago. She earned a B.S. in Broadcast Journalism from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (1987) and a J.D. from DePaul University College of Law (1992). After law school, she founded JDS Mediation Services, Inc. and served as Executive Director of Cook County Justice for Children and the Cook County Justice Advisory Council. She taught conflict management and negotiation skills at Loyola University Chicago, and served as Director of the Center for Public Safety and Justice at University of Illinois at Chicago. Stratton was elected to the Illinois House of Representatives (26th District / later 5th District under redistricting) in 2014, serving 2015–2019 with a focus on the House Judiciary-Criminal Law Committee and criminal justice reform. She was elected Lieutenant Governor of Illinois in 2018 — the first Black woman elected to statewide office in Illinois history — and reelected in 2022. As Lt. Gov., she has led the Governor's Rural Affairs Council and championed cannabis legalization equity provisions. She announced her Senate candidacy in late April 2025, one day after Dick Durbin announced his retirement. Gov. Pritzker endorsed her immediately and has committed $5M to the independent expenditure PAC supporting her (Illinois Future PAC). She pledged not to accept any corporate PAC money for her Senate campaign. She has surged in polling in the final weeks before the March 17 primary, with one poll showing her at 33% (vs. Krishnamoorthi at 29%). (Sources: Ballotpedia; Chicago Sun-Times; WTTW Voter Guide 2026; FEC S6IL00458)
prior_office: Lieutenant Governor of Illinois (2019–present); Illinois State Representative (26th/5th District, 2015–2019)

jacket.note: FEC campaign committee S6IL00458 raised ~$4M directly. Illinois Future PAC — an independent expenditure PAC backing Stratton — has spent at least $11.8M per FEC records (Capitol News Illinois, March 2026). Pritzker committed $5M to Illinois Future PAC; other contributors include Laura Ricketts (Chicago Cubs co-owner, $250K) and multiple Pritzker family members. Stratton pledged no corporate PAC money to her campaign committee — a pledge she has kept to her campaign committee, but critics note she benefits from the Illinois Future PAC super PAC.

donors.name: Gov. J.B. Pritzker (via Illinois Future PAC — independent expenditure)
donors.label: Independent Expenditure PAC — NOT campaign committee funds
donors.amount_note: $5M confirmed to Illinois Future PAC per Chicago Tribune (Feb. 1, 2026). Cannot legally coordinate with campaign.

donors.name: Laura Ricketts (Chicago Cubs co-owner)
donors.label: Contribution to Illinois Future PAC (independent expenditure)
donors.amount_note: Largest single non-Pritzker disclosed contribution to Illinois Future PAC in last two months per Capitol News Illinois

donors.name: Pritzker family members (MK Pritzker, adult children, cousins Adam/John/Gigi/Jennifer Pritzker)
donors.label: Direct individual contributions to campaign committee
donors.amount_note: MK Pritzker, two adult children, and four cousins each contributed the max $7,000 (split primary/general = $3,500 + $3,500 per person). Six family members × $7,000 = $42,000. JB Pritzker himself gave the max $7,000 directly. Jennifer Pritzker is a former Trump supporter who has more recently backed Democrats. (Capitol News Illinois, July 28, 2025)

donors.name: AIPAC-network donors (≥27 individuals — direct campaign committee)
donors.label: Direct contributions to campaign committee
donors.amount_note: The Intercept (March 12, 2026): at least 27 AIPAC donors gave $70K+ directly to Stratton's Senate campaign committee. Lee Rosenberg, former AIPAC president, sits on Stratton's campaign finance committee.

donors.name: Illinois General Assembly members (12 donors)
donors.amount_note: 12 IL General Assembly members contributed to campaign; amounts not aggregated in reporting — requires itemized FEC lookup

donors.name: Jerry Reinsdorf (White Sox and Bulls owner) and family
donors.amount_note: Jerry Reinsdorf, son and Bulls President Michael Reinsdorf each contributed per Capitol News Illinois. Exact amounts require itemized FEC lookup.

donors.name: Danny Wirtz (Blackhawks CEO)
donors.amount_note: Confirmed per Capitol News Illinois (July 28, 2025). Amount requires itemized FEC lookup.

donors.name: Sen. Tammy Duckworth
donors.amount_note: Confirmed per Capitol News Illinois. Amount requires FEC lookup.

donors.name: Marquis Energy Corporate PAC
donors.label: Returned — no longer in campaign funds
donors.amount_note: Stratton's campaign received $5,000 from Marquis Energy Corporate PAC and returned it in June 2025 per Chicago Tribune. However, campaign accepted $21,000 from Marquis family members personally. Marquis family also contributed $46,000 to Level Up PAC (Stratton's hybrid PAC) per Sun-Times.

jacket.donors_note: Stratton pledged no corporate PAC money to her campaign committee — a pledge confirmed by Sun-Times and Tribune. BUT: (1) Her prior state campaign funds accepted corporate PAC money; (2) she accepted $21,000 from Marquis Energy family members individually; (3) Illinois Future PAC — which has spent $11.8M on her behalf — is overwhelmingly funded by billionaire Gov. Pritzker; (4) her own Level Up PAC (created Jan. 2025) accepted $46,000 from Marquis family and $5,000 in corporate PAC money. Krishnamoorthi calls this 'shameless hypocrisy.' (Sources: Chicago Sun-Times 11/21/2025; Chicago Tribune 11/6/2025; Capitol News Illinois 3/10/2026)

red_flags.label: AIPAC Donor Network — $70K+ Direct + Former AIPAC President on Finance Committee
red_flags.detail: The Intercept reported on March 12, 2026 that at least 27 AIPAC donors gave $70K+ directly to Stratton's Senate campaign. Lee Rosenberg, a former president of AIPAC, sits on her campaign finance committee. AIPAC is officially staying neutral in the IL Senate race but its donor network is active for Stratton. This is an apparent tension with her progressive positioning, particularly given that she has been endorsed by Elizabeth Warren and End Citizens United.

red_flags.label: Corporate Contributions Despite 'No Corporate PAC' Pledge — Sun-Times Investigation
red_flags.detail: Chicago Sun-Times (Nov. 21, 2025) reported that while Stratton vowed to reject corporate PAC money, she has a history of accepting corporate PAC and direct corporate contributions in her state campaign funds since 2016. For her Senate race: she returned a $5,000 Marquis Energy PAC check but accepted $21,000 from Marquis family members personally, and her Level Up PAC accepted $46,000 from Marquis family and $5,000 in corporate PAC money. Krishnamoorthi's campaign aired a TV ad citing the Sun-Times story. Stratton's campaign says the pledge applies only to her Senate campaign committee, which has held to it.

red_flags.label: Illinois Future PAC — $11.8M in Outside Spending, Pritzker-Controlled
red_flags.detail: Illinois Future PAC, which has spent $11.8M supporting Stratton and attacking Krishnamoorthi, is overwhelmingly funded by Gov. JB Pritzker ($5M) and allies including Laura Ricketts ($250K). Stratton benefits directly from this spending while officially not coordinating. The Fairshake PAC's ads and the Indian American Impact Fund PAC have attacked Stratton for 'relying on dark money super PACs.' Capitol News Illinois (March 10, 2026) confirmed Illinois Future PAC as the largest outside spender in the race.

red_flags.label: Democratic Lieutenant Governors Association (DLGA) PAC — ICE Contractor Funding
red_flags.detail: Fairshake PAC ads accused Stratton of benefiting from the Democratic Lieutenant Governors Association PAC, which has received money from an ICE contractor. Krishnamoorthi's campaign cited this to counter Stratton's attacks on him for accepting Palantir CTO donations. Capitol News Illinois confirmed the DLGA PAC connection. The allegation is that one of Stratton's PAC supporters is funded by the same type of ICE contractor she attacks Krishnamoorthi for. Confirmed as a claim in the race — exact details of DLGA donor composition require independent verification.

key_votes.summary: As IL State Representative, Stratton was a lead advocate for HB 40, ensuring Illinois women retain access to abortion funding regardless of insurance coverage if Roe v. Wade were overturned. Signed into law October 2017.

key_votes.bill: Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (Illinois SB 1557), 2019
key_votes.summary: As Lt. Governor, Stratton championed Illinois's landmark cannabis legalization law, including equity provisions for expungements and minority business licensing. She led the Governor's Justice, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion (JEDI) Cannabis Commission. Signed January 1, 2020.

key_votes.bill: SAFE-T Act (Illinois SB 2328) — Criminal Justice Reform, 2021
key_votes.summary: Stratton publicly supported and advocated for the SAFE-T Act, Illinois's comprehensive criminal justice reform package: ended cash bail, established police accountability measures, reformed use-of-force standards. Signed February 2021.

key_votes.bill: Illinois SALT Act — Protecting Residents from Federal Immigration Enforcement
key_votes.summary: Stratton cited the SALT Act (signed under Pritzker administration in 2025) as protecting Illinoisans from 'illegal and unethical federal immigration enforcement conduct.' She listed it as a key accomplishment in her Sun-Times candidate questionnaire (March 2026).

As Lt. Governor, Stratton co-signed the FY2025 state budget, which included expanded mental health funding, SNAP support, affordable housing investment, and clean energy programs. Standard executive role.

endorsements.name: International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers (IAM)
Carol Moseley Braun (Former U.S. Senator, IL)
IL Senate Majority Leader Kimberly Lightford
IL House Majority Leader Robyn Gabel
Jesse White (Former Illinois Secretary of State)

career_history.highlight: First Black woman elected to statewide office in Illinois history; reelected 2022; chairs Governor's Rural Affairs Council; led JEDI Cannabis Commission for equitable cannabis licensing; sued Trump administration to defend federal funding for child care, SNAP, infrastructure, and disaster relief
House Judiciary-Criminal Law Committee; lead advocate for HB 40 (Reproductive Health Act, 2017) and criminal justice reform; championed SAFE-T Act groundwork

career_history.role: Director, Center for Public Safety and Justice
career_history.highlight: Academic and policy research role in criminal justice
Taught conflict management and negotiation skills

career_history.org: Cook County Justice for Children / Cook County Justice Advisory Council
career_history.highlight: Led county juvenile justice advisory work; positioned her as a criminal justice reform expert
Founded private mediation firm after law school — background in conflict resolution and legal services
Law degree; focus on social justice and legal advocacy

policy_platform.position: Championed sentencing reform, rehabilitation over punishment, and reducing mass incarceration. Led SAFE-T Act advocacy as Lt. Gov. Supports ending cash bail nationally. Campaigning on a 'People First' platform against mass incarceration.
Led HB 40 (2017) to ensure IL abortion access regardless of insurance. Supports codifying Roe v. Wade at federal level. EMILYs List endorsement.

Supports Medicare for All; specifically supports a federal Birth Equity Initiative to address maternal mortality (inspired by Illinois program). 'Tax revenue should be spent on quality, accessible health care.' (Sun-Times questionnaire, March 2026)
Supports abolishing ICE (per WTTW debate, March 9, 2026 — confirmed by Illinois Future PAC ad messaging). Supports SALT Act-style protections for immigrants at federal level.

Pledged no corporate PAC money to Senate campaign committee; endorsed by End Citizens United. Campaigning explicitly against the corporate PAC money model as distinguishing her from Krishnamoorthi and Kelly.

Supported Illinois cannabis legalization (SB 1557, 2019). Chaired the JEDI Cannabis Commission for equitable licensing and expungement. Supports federal cannabis decriminalization with equity provisions.

Supports expanding Earned Income Tax Credit; Medicare for All; SNAP expansion; affordable housing investment. Framing: 'When [vulnerable populations] thrive, our whole community thrives.' Backed by SEIU, teachers unions, Teamsters.

social_pulse.summary: Surging in final weeks — one poll shows her at 33%, ahead of Krishnamoorthi (29%). Strong earned media from Warren, EMILYs List, and progressive organizations. Negative ads from Fairshake ($5.5M+) attacking her record, AIPAC Intercept story, and Krishnamoorthi 'hypocrisy' attacks have created friction. Positive national coverage as progressive alternative. Base is Chicago South Side and south suburbs (overlapping with Kelly's base), suburban Democrats, and progressive women voters.

trust_indicators.label: First Black woman elected to statewide office in Illinois history
No personal ethics violations on record
Consistent progressive voting record in General Assembly
No corporate PAC money in Senate campaign committee (pledge honored)
Endorsed by EMILYs List, End Citizens United, Sierra Club, NNU, SEIU, IFT, Teamsters
$11.8M in outside PAC support via Illinois Future PAC (Pritzker-controlled billionaire money)
AIPAC donor network: $70K+ direct donations + former AIPAC president on finance committee (Intercept, March 2026)
Prior history of corporate PAC acceptance in state campaign funds (Sun-Times investigation)
Accepted $21K from Marquis Energy family members despite returning their PAC check
Chicago Tribune Editorial Board did NOT endorse Stratton for Senate primary

news_hits.headline: AIPAC Is Staying Out of Illinois Senate Race — But Its Donors Back Juliana Stratton
news_hits.summary: The Intercept reported that at least 27 AIPAC donors gave $70K+ directly to Stratton's campaign committee, and that former AIPAC president Lee Rosenberg sits on her campaign finance committee — despite AIPAC officially remaining neutral in the race.

news_hits.headline: The crypto lobby has poured $8.6M into Illinois primaries — $5.2M in single ad buy targeting Stratton
news_hits.summary: Fairshake, the crypto industry super PAC funded primarily by Coinbase and Trump megadonors Andreessen Horowitz, dropped $5.2 million in a single ad buy attacking Stratton — making her one of the most crypto-targeted Senate candidates in the country this cycle.

news_hits.headline: Crypto PACs flood Illinois Senate primary — ads accuse Stratton of ties to Madigan and ICE contractor
news_hits.summary: Fairshake ads accuse Stratton of being aligned with convicted former IL House Speaker Michael Madigan, and allege one of her supporting PACs accepted money from an ICE contractor — charges Stratton and allies deny.

news_hits.headline: A historic Senate opening meets a divided Illinois Democratic Party
news_hits.summary: Politico's pre-primary analysis notes that crypto PACs are running attack ads against Stratton while simultaneously boosting Kelly to siphon votes away from her — a coordinated strategy to benefit Krishnamoorthi.

news_hits.headline: Will Illinois' Democratic Primary for Senate Divide Black Voters?
news_hits.summary: The New York Times examines how the three-way race — with Stratton and Kelly splitting the Black female electorate — could hand Krishnamoorthi a plurality win despite neither progressive candidate being able to consolidate support.

news_hits.headline: As Illinois Election Day 2026 nears, Senate race candidates Juliana Stratton, Raja Krishnamoorthi try to win undecided voters
news_hits.title: As Illinois Election Day 2026 nears, Senate race candidates Juliana Stratton, Raja Krishnamoorthi try to win undecided voters
news_hits.summary: ABC7 Chicago (March 12) reports Stratton's most recent poll showed she was holding a slight lead, while Krishnamoorthi countered with his own poll suggesting he was in front. Race too close to call heading into final weekend.

news_hits.headline: A voter's guide to the confusing world of super PACs influencing 2026 Illinois primary races
news_hits.title: A voter's guide to the confusing world of super PACs influencing 2026 Illinois primary races
news_hits.summary: WBEZ (March 13) explains the super PAC landscape. Gov. Pritzker has contributed $5M+ to the pro-Stratton super PAC which has spent $11.8M total. Notes Krishnamoorthi's allied PAC has also run ads designed to boost Kelly at Stratton's expense.

news_hits.headline: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ pre-primary overview (March 13) covers Stratton's final pitch, including her viral 'f*** Trump' advertisement and surge in advertising in the final weeks.

news_hits.headline: Illinois Senate race yields intense Democratic primary battle
news_hits.title: Illinois Senate race yields intense Democratic primary battle
news_hits.summary: The Hill's March 14 eve-of-primary recap covers the intense three-way Democratic battle for Dick Durbin's seat. Krishnamoorthi, Stratton, and Kelly are blitzing ads and drawing support from national figures as they barrel toward Tuesday's March 17 showdown. Notes early voting turnout could approach 30%, well above typical midterm primaries.

news_hits.headline: Juliana Stratton lays out her case in 2026 Illinois Senate primary
news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton lays out her case in 2026 Illinois Senate primary
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago profiles Stratton's closing argument, noting her recent poll showing a slight lead and Gov. Pritzker's active campaigning on her behalf. She is battling both Krishnamoorthi's money advantage and the presence of Robin Kelly splitting Black voters.

news_hits.headline: Illinois' US Senate race is a test of Gov. JB Pritzker's political influence
news_hits.title: Illinois' US Senate race is a test of Gov. JB Pritzker's political influence
news_hits.summary: AP News (March 12) examines whether Pritzker's endorsement of Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton will translate to a win. Pritzker was photographed campaigning with Stratton at a Chicago senior residence on March 12. His ability to deliver a Senate seat is seen as a gauge of his political capital ahead of a potential 2028 presidential run.

news_hits.title: On final weekend before Illinois primary, candidates try to keep it local despite flood of out-of-state money
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune's final-weekend dispatch covers candidates canvassing the South Side Irish Parade, Black churches, and local rallies on the eve of the March 17 primary. Preckwinkle, Reilly, Krishnamoorthi, Kelly, and Stratton all named; the piece emphasizes how tens of millions in out-of-state PAC money (pro-Israel AIPAC, crypto, AI interests) have nationalized what were traditionally local races.

news_hits.title: Illinois' Senate Democratic primary a lesson in campaign finance and a test of Gov. JB Pritzker's power
news_hits.summary: Tribune's definitive pre-primary Senate analysis: the contest has narrowed to Krishnamoorthi, Kelly, and Stratton. The race is framed as a test of Gov. Pritzker's political power through his backing of Stratton, his two-term running mate. Notes the cascade of outside PAC money since Durbin's retirement announcement and the role of Trump's second term (immigration raids, Iran war, federal funding threats) as the backdrop.

news_hits.title: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton makes a final pitch for U.S. Senate before March 17 primary
news_hits.summary: WBEZ In The Loop interview with Stratton as she closes out the Senate campaign. NYT analysis showed all three leading candidates in a tight race as Stratton appeared to be gaining momentum in final weeks.

news_hits.title: Raja Krishnamoorthi, Juliana Stratton and Robin Kelly stick to script in Senate race's final TV debate
news_hits.summary: Final debate before the March 17 primary. Stratton and Krishnamoorthi clashed over financial backers; Stratton pressed Krishnamoorthi on whether corporate and pro-Israel PAC money would compromise his independence from Trump.

news_hits.title: As Senate race tightens, Kelly benefits from super PAC war between Krishnamoorthi, Stratton
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times reports pro-Krishnamoorthi super PAC forces are boosting Robin Kelly with ads designed to drain votes from Stratton, who appeared to be gaining momentum. The strategy underscores the intensifying three-way dynamic with one week left.

news_hits.title: What to expect in the Illinois state primary
news_hits.summary: AP Decision Notes preview of the March 17 Illinois primary. Senate race: Kelly, Krishnamoorthi, and Stratton (Pritzker-endorsed) top the Democratic field; Krishnamoorthi leads in fundraising with ~$6.6M cash on hand. Pritzker is unopposed for Democratic governor renomination. Republican governor field includes multiple challengers trying to prevent Pritzker's third term.

news_hits.title: 4 things to watch Tuesday in Illinois primaries, including a hotly contested Senate battle
news_hits.summary: Election-eve preview from STLPR covering Illinois primary watchpoints. Senate race (Kelly/Krishnamoorthi/Stratton) is top race; candidates have diverged on minimum wage ($25/hr Stratton vs. $17/hr Kelly and Krishnamoorthi). Race has been contentious over MAGA-aligned and corporate donor accusations between Krishnamoorthi and Stratton.

news_hits.title: Cold weather to greet Illinois voters Tuesday as primary features open US Senate, crowded House races
news_hits.summary: Tribune election-eve dispatch from March 16 covers the final day before the primary. Pritzker and Stratton campaign signs are shown together outside early voting sites, underscoring the governor's active campaigning for his Lt. Governor's Senate bid. Cold weather forecast for the Chicago area may affect turnout.

news_hits.title: What to Watch in Tuesday's Illinois Primaries — and Where the Money Went
news_hits.summary: NYT Election Day guide names Stratton as a top-tier contender in the IL Senate primary. Late polling shows her surging to 29.4% vs Krishnamoorthi's 31.6% — within the margin of error, making the race a genuine toss-up.

news_hits.title: Illinois Election Live Updates: Voting Underway in Senate Primary to Succeed Durbin
news_hits.summary: Juliana Stratton, backed by Gov. JB Pritzker and the state's political machine, faces Krishnamoorthi and Kelly in the Senate primary; stepped down as Lt. Gov. to run.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Map: Live Election Results
news_hits.summary: FairShake crypto PAC spent more than $5M on ads attacking Stratton's campaign. Christian Mitchell, Pritzker's former deputy governor, declared candidacy for Lt. Gov. after Stratton stepped aside.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois primary day election results
news_hits.summary: Stratton among top Democratic contenders in IL US Senate primary race to succeed retiring Dick Durbin.

news_hits.title: Election Night: Stratton leads Illinois Senate Dem primary with 39% at 54% counted
news_hits.summary: With 54% of Illinois Democratic primary votes counted as of 9:16 PM ET, Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton leads the U.S. Senate race with 266,114 votes (39%), ahead of Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi at 32.6% and Rep. Robin Kelly at 20.3%. Stratton holds a +10 advantage in Cook County (78% in) but trails in suburban collar counties. Pritzker's $5M super PAC backing and her 9-point Cook County edge are powering her lead.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton defeats Raja Krishnamoorthi in Senate Democratic primary battle for Dick Durbin's seat
news_hits.summary: Stratton wins with momentum from Gov. Pritzker's $5M PAC support and her bold anti-ICE stance, defeating Krishnamoorthi 39.7%-33.4% with Kelly at 18.4%. 'We did it tonight. We showed what's possible when you listen to the people,' Stratton said at her election night party in North Lawndale.

news_hits.title: Stratton wins Illinois primary, giving Dems another Black female senator
news_hits.summary: Stratton's victory is framed nationally as a historic win — she will be on track to become Illinois' fourth Black senator and the sixth Black woman in the current Senate if she wins in November against Republican Don Tracy.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton wins Illinois Democratic Senate primary race
news_hits.summary: In her victory speech, Stratton promised Medicare for All, a $25/hour minimum wage, abolishing ICE, and to 'pull democracy back from the brink.' Guardian notes Stratton trailed in polling until recent weeks, when Pritzker cash and hardening anti-immigration sentiment powered her surge.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Election Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC's Power Is Tested
news_hits.summary: NYT analysis frames Stratton's win as a test case for progressive anti-Trump messaging in 2026 midterms. Her calls to abolish ICE resonated in Chicago communities affected by Trump's Operation Midway Blitz. She now faces Republican Don Tracy in November for the safely Democratic seat.

news_hits.title: Election Live Updates: Stratton Calls to 'Abolish ICE' After Illinois Primary Win
news_hits.summary: NYT live blog tracks Stratton's victory speech in which she called to abolish ICE, alongside Republican Don Tracy winning the GOP Senate nomination. Stratton is now heavily favored in the November general election for this reliably Democratic seat.

news_hits.title: Stratton, Illinois Senate Primary Winner, Promises a 'Fight' With Trump
news_hits.summary: NYT post-victory profile of Stratton's Senate primary win. She vowed to bring 'this fight straight to Donald Trump's door' and reiterated her call for Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer to step aside from his leadership role. Stratton said she had not heard from Schumer since her victory, though he released a congratulatory statement. Krishnamoorthi conceded at his watch party, saying he would offer 'full support on the road ahead.'

news_hits.title: Illinois Lt. Gov. Stratton wins Senate Democratic primary for Dick Durbin's seat
news_hits.summary: CNBC national coverage of Stratton's Democratic Senate primary win. Covers her victory speech calling to 'abolish ICE' to cheering supporters, her background as the first Black woman elected to statewide office in Illinois history, and the contest as a test of Gov. Pritzker's political power. She defeated 10 other Democratic candidates including Krishnamoorthi and Robin Kelly.

news_hits.title: Illinois election results 2026: Juliana Stratton wins Democratic Senate primary
news_hits.summary: The 19th News covers Stratton's Senate primary win from a gender and racial equity lens. Notes she will now face former Illinois Republican Party chair Don Tracy in November's general election. Stratton thanked fellow candidates in her victory speech and pivoted immediately to the general election.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton's strong suburban, downstate numbers helped fuel her Senate primary victory
news_hits.summary: Analysis of Stratton's primary victory geography: she won 44 of Chicago's 50 wards and performed strongly in suburban Cook County and downstate Illinois, with broad support across Black, Latino, and white voters.

news_hits.title: Gov. JB Pritzker flexes political muscle through Juliana Stratton's decisive Senate primary win
news_hits.summary: How Pritzker's endorsement and financial support was the decisive factor in Stratton's Senate primary victory. The story examines Stratton's path forward to the general election and the political dynamics of the Pritzker-Stratton ticket.

news_hits.title: King of Illinois: Pritzker swings Senate race as he targets Trump
news_hits.summary: Politico covers how Pritzker's support lifted Stratton to the Democratic Senate nomination, with national implications for the Illinois governor's political future.

news_hits.title: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton looking to make history in US Senate race against ex-Illinois GOP chair Don Tracy
news_hits.summary: Tribune post-primary profile frames the November general election matchup: Stratton vs. Republican nominee Don Tracy, former Illinois Republican Party chair. If she defeats Tracy, Stratton would become just the sixth Black woman — and second from Illinois — elected to the U.S. Senate. The race is rated solidly Democratic.

news_hits.title: How Illinois voted in the US Senate race
news_hits.summary: Tribune geographic analysis of Stratton's March 17 primary win. The AP called the race for Stratton at 9:38 p.m. Don Tracy won the Republican nomination, defeating five other GOP hopefuls. Race now moves to a November general election rematch in a safely Democratic state.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton wins Illinois Senate primary, now heads to general election
news_hits.summary: WBEZ post-primary coverage of Stratton's Senate Democratic primary victory. She faces a likely Republican opponent in November 2026. Stratton's win was powered by Gov. Pritzker's $5M investment through Illinois Future PAC, and she defeated Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi and Rep. Robin Kelly in a three-way race. The piece frames the general election as a likely Democratic hold given Illinois's strong Democratic lean.

news_hits.title: Stratton defeats Krishnamoorthi with help from Chicago, downstate voters
news_hits.summary: Post-primary analysis: Stratton's coalition included Chicago and downstate voters; Krishnamoorthi conceded after 9:30 PM and pledged full support for her general election campaign.

news_hits.title: Stratton defeats Krishnamoorthi with help from Chicago, downstate voters
news_hits.summary: Detailed Capitol News Illinois post-primary breakdown of Stratton's Senate Democratic primary win. Stratton built a 20-point advantage in Chicago over Krishnamoorthi and also outperformed expectations downstate. Analysis credits Pritzker's late $5M infusion to Illinois Future PAC and coordinated labor/party ground game for the win.

news_hits.title: More than $30M in outside spending shaped Illinois primaries, but wins were a split for big-monied interests
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune post-primary spending analysis covers Stratton's Senate win as the most significant outcome of the $30M+ outside money race. Pritzker's $5M investment in Stratton is cited as the decisive outside intervention of the cycle, with the piece noting AIPAC's losses in IL-07 and the Senate race contrasted with wins in IL-02 and IL-08.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, La Shawn Ford and other Illinois primary winners who beat deeper pockets
news_hits.summary: Capitol Fax columnist Rich Miller analyzes the March 17 primary upsets, leading with Juliana Stratton's Senate primary victory over the heavily-funded Raja Krishnamoorthi. Krishnamoorthi spent an estimated $29M on TV ads and had $10M in crypto-industry PAC backing against Stratton. Miller notes Stratton's broader coalition and message discipline overcame a 3-to-1 spending disadvantage.

news_hits.title: 'He has to justify what he did': Black leaders slam JB Pritzker after Illinois primary — casting shadow over Stratton's win
news_hits.summary: Congressional Black Caucus members are venting frustration that Pritzker's financial intervention tilted the Senate primary toward Stratton and away from Robin Kelly, the CBC's endorsed candidate. The CBC's Yvette Clarke called it 'beyond frustrating.' While a win for Stratton, the controversy adds a party unity headache heading into the general election against Republican Don Tracy — with CBC members signaling they won't forget the slight going into 2028.

news_hits.title: Gov. JB Pritzker flexes political muscle through Juliana Stratton's decisive Senate primary win
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times post-primary analysis of Pritzker's role in Stratton's Senate primary win. Notes Stratton faces Republican nominee Don Tracy (former Illinois Republican Party chair) in November in deep-blue Illinois — giving her a clear path toward becoming only the sixth Black woman elected to the U.S. Senate.

news_hits.title: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton wins Democratic primary for U.S. Senate
news_hits.summary: PBS NewsHour reports Juliana Stratton won Tuesday's Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, edging out two sitting members of the U.S. House — Reps. Krishnamoorthi and Kelly — to advance to a November general election against Republican nominee Don Tracy, the former state party chair. If she wins in November, Stratton would become the 15th Black U.S. senator in American history.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton wins Illinois Senate Democratic primary election race
news_hits.summary: NBC News projects Juliana Stratton as the winner of the Illinois Democratic Senate primary. She will face former Illinois Republican Party Chairman Don Tracy, whom NBC News projects as the Republican Senate primary winner, in the November general election. The seat, held by retiring Sen. Dick Durbin since 1997, is expected to remain Democratic.

news_hits.title: Congressional Black Caucus chair offers curt congratulations to Stratton
news_hits.summary: CBC chair gave a measured congratulation to Stratton after her Senate primary win. The CBC had backed Rep. Robin Kelly and warned Gov. Pritzker against his millions in support for Stratton — creating friction heading into the general election campaign.

news_hits.title: Takeaways from the Illinois primary elections
news_hits.summary: CNN post-primary analysis: Stratton's win was also a win for Gov. JB Pritzker, while big-spending PAC groups got mixed results in Illinois contests. Crypto PAC that spent $10M against Stratton lost; AIPAC-aligned spending had mixed outcomes.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton faces Republican Don Tracy in Illinois Senate general election
news_hits.summary: General election for Illinois U.S. Senate confirmed as Juliana Stratton (D) vs. Don Tracy (R), plus independents Austin Mink, Tyrone Muhammad, and Anthony Smith on the November 3, 2026 ballot. Republicans have not won a statewide Illinois election in more than a decade; Stratton is the heavy favorite.

news_hits.title: 2026 United States Senate election in Illinois: Stratton wins primary with 40.1%
news_hits.summary: Final certified primary results: Juliana Stratton won the Democratic Senate nomination with 40.1% of the vote, defeating Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi and Rep. Robin Kelly. Republican Don Tracy won the GOP primary with 39.9% of the vote. Stratton will be the heavy favorite in November — Democrats have not lost a Senate race in Illinois since 2010.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton's strong suburban, downstate coalition fueled her Senate primary victory — Sun-Times analysis
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times geographic analysis of the March 17 primary results shows Stratton won 44 of Chicago's 50 wards and led across majority-Black (50%), Latino (41%) and white (41%) wards. She led in suburban Cook County by a thin 35.9%-35.4% margin over Krishnamoorthi, and outperformed expectations downstate (41.4% to Krishnamoorthi's 36.4%). Her coalition was broader than her campaign had anticipated, aided significantly by Gov. Pritzker's $5M PAC support.

news_hits.title: Politicians battle over immigration policy after Loyola student's death — Stratton's general election positioning under pressure
news_hits.summary: The murder of Loyola University student Sheridan Gorman by a Venezuelan migrant who had prior contact with Chicago police triggered a political firestorm on March 25. Democrats including Stratton, who won the Senate primary on an anti-Trump immigration messaging platform, now face pressure to defend sanctuary city policies ahead of the general election. Republican Senate challenger Don Tracy and gubernatorial candidate Darren Bailey are using Gorman's death to attack Illinois Democrats on crime and immigration enforcement — a direct challenge to Stratton's core general election narrative.
